steps.title: Read the snowfall values from the graph
steps.content: The labeled points on the line graph show that in 2003, the annual snowfall was 40 inches, and in 2007, it was 10 inches.

steps.title: Calculate the percent decrease
steps.content:
Substitute the values into the percent decrease formula:
$$p = \frac{40 - 10}{40} \times 100$$
Simplify:
$$p = \frac{30}{40} \times 100 = \frac{3}{4} \times 100 = 75$$
The value of p is 75.
